Title: ChLae1 and ChVel1 Regulate T-toxin Production, Virulence, Oxidative Stress Response, and Development of the Maize Pathogen Cochliobolus heterostrophus

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PKS18 expression is associated with enhanced melanization in Chlae1 and Chvel1 mutants; reduced PKS18 expression is associated with reduced melanization in ChLAE1 or ChVEL1 overexpression strains.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR of PKS18; melanization phenotyping on solid and liquid media; overexpression analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during mycelial melanization, elevated at 40–48 h in Chlae1/Chvel1 mutants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: constant light or dark; liquid culture; PGA-induced overexpression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKS18 transcript levels increased strongly in Chlae1 and Chvel1 mutants during mycelial melanization. 2. PKS18 expression decreased in most ChLAE1 and ChVEL1 overexpression strains. 3. PKS18 is a key readout of negative regulation of melanin biosynthesis by ChLae1/ChVel1.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PKS18 is central to melanin biosynthesis, it was not the primary manipulated gene in this paper; it was analyzed mainly as a downstream target/readout.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron acquisition, oxidative stress tolerance, and virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NPS6 expression under low iron was unaffected in Chlae1 and Chvel1 mutants, indicating ChLae1/ChVel1 do not regulate this siderophore pathway at the transcriptional level tested.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparison with nps6 deletion phenotype; qPCR/induction analysis under low iron
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced under iron-depleted conditions in WT and mutants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: iron depletion; oxidative stress comparison with H2O2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPS6 is strongly induced by low iron. 2. This induction is unchanged in Chlae1 and Chvel1 mutants. 3. ChLae1 and ChVel1 are therefore not major transcriptional regulators of NPS6 under the tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because extracellular siderophore metabolism was a central comparative metabolite in the study, but NPS6 itself was not the primary experimental subject.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: oxidative stress tolerance relevant to pathogenic fitness
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CAT3 expression is strongly reduced in the Chvel1 mutant, correlating with increased H2O2 sensitivity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR after H2O2 treatment; oxidative stress growth assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in Chvel1 mutant at 0 min and 30 min after H2O2 addition
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAT3 was the only surveyed oxidative-stress gene with major expression change in Chvel1. 2. CAT3 expression dropped about 20-fold before and about 4-fold after H2O2 treatment in Chvel1. 3. Reduced CAT3 likely contributes to Chvel1 oxidative stress sensitivity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream target/readout rather than primary focal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes high virulence to Texas male sterile cytoplasm maize
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported by qPCR as core biosynthetic genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PKS1 expression is induced in dark in WT but greatly reduced in Chlae1 and especially Chvel1 mutants; increased in overexpression strains, paralleling T-toxin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR/qPCR expression profiling in mutants and overexpression lines; microbial T-toxin bioassay; complementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Minimal in light, induced in dark in WT; not properly induced in Chlae1/Chvel1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKS1 is dark-induced in WT. 2. Dark induction is lost or strongly reduced in Chvel1 and Chlae1 mutants. 3. PKS1 expression increases in ChLAE1 and modestly in ChVEL1 overexpression strains, consistent with elevated T-toxin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PKS1 was analyzed as a core Tox1 biosynthetic marker, but the direct experimental manipulation centered on ChLAE1 and ChVEL1.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes high virulence to Texas male sterile cytoplasm maize
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported by qPCR as core biosynthetic genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PKS2 expression is induced in dark in WT but greatly reduced in Chlae1 and especially Chvel1 mutants; increased in overexpression strains, paralleling T-toxin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR/qPCR expression profiling in mutants and overexpression lines; microbial T-toxin bioassay; complementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Minimal in light, induced in dark in WT; not properly induced in Chlae1/Chvel1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKS2 is dark-induced in WT. 2. Dark induction is largely abolished in Chvel1 and reduced in Chlae1 mutants. 3. PKS2 expression increases in ChLAE1 and modestly in ChVEL1 overexpression strains.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PKS2 was a major readout for T-toxin pathway regulation rather than a directly manipulated focal gene.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal culture and infected maize tissue
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: confers super virulence to Texas male sterile cytoplasm maize
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of T-toxin biosynthesis; negative regulation of DHN melanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of Tox1 genes; transcriptional repression of melanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PKS1; PKS2; DEC1; RED1; RED2; RED3; TOX9; OXI1; LAM1; PKS18; CMR1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates all examined Tox1 biosynthetic genes especially under dark conditions; represses PKS18 and CMR1 associated with melanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of T-toxin production and virulence; negative regulator of melanin biosynthesis and asexual sporulation; promotes oxidative stress tolerance, aerial hyphal growth, and female fertility.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; complementation; overexpression under pelA promoter; RT-PCR/qPCR; microbial toxin bioassay; maize infection assay; H2O2 sensitivity assay; melanization assays; reproductive phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pelA promoter from Aspergillus nidulans, inducible by polygalacturonic acid and repressible by glucose
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: constant light versus constant dark; 19°C for toxin assay; PGA induction; H2O2 treatment; maize infection
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increases T-toxin and chlorosis on T-cms maize but reduces lesion size on N-cytoplasm maize, consistent with fitness cost of T-toxin production; overexpression also causes female sterility.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: First identified regulator shown to control host-selective T-toxin biosynthesis in C. heterostrophus; strongly elevates toxin production when overexpressed, especially overriding light repression.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely acts with ChVel1 in a velvet-related complex; discussion suggests possible interaction with additional velvet proteins such as VelB/VosA homologs
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Stoichiometric balance with ChVel1 appears important; ChLAE1 overexpression can disturb light/dark regulation and reduce ChVEL1 expression on some media
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Native expression detectable in WT; overexpression strains show clear upshift; function prominent under dark for toxin control but OE causes strong light expression of Tox1 genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of ChLAE1 reduces T-toxin production and weakens dark induction of all nine Tox1 genes. 2. Overexpression of ChLAE1 causes profuse T-toxin overproduction in both light and dark and increases virulence symptoms related to T-toxin on T-cms maize. 3. ChLAE1 also represses melanin biosynthesis, represses asexual sporulation, promotes aerial hyphal growth, oxidative stress tolerance, and female fertility.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for reducing T-toxin-mediated virulence in maize pathosystems; manipulation could suppress host-selective toxin production, but pleiotropic effects on development and stress responses should be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper establishes ChLAE1 as one of the first factors known to regulate host-selective toxin production. It also suggests ChLae1 may participate in age-dependent and light-responsive toxin regulation beyond simple ChLae1-ChVel1 partnership.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: VeA/Velvet family protein with predicted monopartite NLS and PEST motif
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal culture and infected maize tissue
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: confers super virulence to Texas male sterile cytoplasm maize
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of T-toxin biosynthesis; negative regulation of DHN melanin biosynthesis; regulation of oxidative stress-associated CAT3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of Tox1 genes and CAT3; transcriptional repression of PKS18 and CMR1; developmental repression of conidiation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PKS1; PKS2; DEC1; RED1; RED2; RED3; TOX9; OXI1; LAM1; CAT3; PKS18; CMR1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Tox1 genes in dark; activates CAT3 expression; represses PKS18 and CMR1 expression associated with melanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of T-toxin production, virulence, oxidative stress tolerance, sexual development, and aerial hyphal growth; negative regulator of melanin biosynthesis and asexual sporulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; complementation; overexpression under pelA promoter; RT-PCR/qPCR; microbial toxin bioassay; maize infection assay; H2O2 sensitivity assay; melanization assays; reproductive phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pelA promoter from Aspergillus nidulans, inducible by polygalacturonic acid and repressible by glucose
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: constant light versus constant dark; 19°C for toxin assay; PGA induction; H2O2 treatment; maize infection
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increases T-toxin and chlorosis on T-cms maize but reduces lesion size on N-cytoplasm maize, consistent with fitness cost of T-toxin production.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: First identified regulator shown to control host-selective T-toxin biosynthesis; dark-specific positive regulator consistent with velvet/light signaling logic.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely functions in a velvet complex with ChLae1; discussion references possible VelB/VosA-related complexes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: ChVEL1 overexpression modestly increases ChLAE1 expression; function depends on light-responsive regulatory context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression strains show clear upshift; strongest impact on Tox1 induction occurs in dark
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of ChVEL1 nearly abolishes dark induction of Tox1 genes and strongly reduces T-toxin production. 2. ChVEL1 overexpression modestly increases T-toxin production and Tox1 expression in dark but not light. 3. ChVEL1 also promotes oxidative stress tolerance partly through CAT3, represses melanin biosynthesis, represses conidiation, and enhances female fertility.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to attenuate fungal virulence and host-selective toxin production in maize disease management; pleiotropic control of development and stress adaptation makes it a high-value but broad-effect intervention point.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with ChLAE1, ChVEL1 has a stronger dark-dependent pattern and a clearer link to CAT3-mediated oxidative stress tolerance.
